steps.title: Set off the contrast
steps.content: The first independent clause says the beach's sand is a more unusual shade. Though works as a trailing contrast word in that clause, and the next independent clause explains why the sand is pink.

steps.title: Reject splice errors
steps.content: A and B join two independent clauses with only commas. C places though at the start of the second clause, making the structure awkward and incomplete after the semicolon. The remaining choice sets off though and then uses a semicolon between the clauses. The answer is D.
